data.description_suggestion: Creates a new payment order in the Pay.nl Transaction Gateway. Accepts order details including amount, currency, and merchant reference via CreateOrderRequest. Returns the created Order object with a unique order ID and payment links for processing. The order is initialized in a pending state and ready for payment processing.
data.reasoning: Description is completely empty. Added context about what the operation does, what inputs it expects (CreateOrderRequest), what it returns (Order object with ID and links), and the initial state of the created order for AI agent clarity.

data.description_suggestion: Approves an order identified by its ID, transitioning it from pending to approved state. This operation authorizes the order for payment processing. Returns the updated Order object with status changed to approved. Only orders in pending state can be approved.

message: Operation 'declineorder' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Description is empty. data.description_suggestion: Declines an order identified by its ID, transitioning it to declined state. This operation rejects the order and halts payment processing. Returns the updated Order object with status changed to declined. Only orders in pending or approved state can be declined.

data.description_suggestion: Captures an approved order identified by its ID, finalizing the payment transaction. data.current_summary: Capture a specific amount for an order
data.description_suggestion: Captures a specified monetary amount from an authorized order, transitioning it from authorized to captured state. Requires the order ID and the amount to capture in the request body. Returns the updated order object with the new capture status and remaining balance. This operation is typically used in two-step payment processing workflows where authorization precedes capture.

Added context about the operation's purpose in payment processing, required inputs (order ID and amount), expected output (updated order object), and the state transition that occurs. data.current_summary: Capture specific products for an order
data.description_suggestion: Captures payment for specific products within an order, enabling partial fulfillment scenarios. Requires the order ID and a list of product identifiers or line items to capture in the request body. Returns the updated order object reflecting the captured products and any remaining uncaptured items. Useful for orders with multiple products where items are fulfilled and captured at different times.

message: Operation 'voidorder' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, poor_ai_interpretability. Summary is too generic. Description is missing entirely. Enhanced summary to clarify this is for authorized orders specifically. Added description explaining the void operation's purpose (canceling authorization), required inputs, expected output, and the business context (releasing held funds, used before capture). This helps AI agents distinguish void from abort and understand the payment state implications.

data.description_suggestion: Voids an authorized order, canceling the authorization hold without capturing funds. Requires the order ID as a path parameter. Returns the updated order object with void status. This operation is used to cancel authorized transactions before capture, releasing the held funds back to the customer. Voiding is typically performed on orders that will not be fulfilled.
data.reasoning: Summary is too generic. Description is missing entirely. Enhanced summary to clarify this is for authorized orders specifically. Added description explaining the void operation's purpose (canceling authorization), required inputs, expected output, and the business context (releasing held funds, used before capture). This helps AI agents distinguish void from abort and understand the payment state implications.

message: Operation 'abortorder' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, unclear_purpose, poor_ai_interpretability. Summary is too generic. Description is missing entirely. Enhanced summary for clarity. Added description explaining abort's purpose (canceling before authorization), required inputs, expected output, and the key distinction from void (abort happens before authorization, void after). This helps AI agents understand the order lifecycle and when to use abort versus void.

data.description_suggestion: Aborts an order transaction, canceling it before authorization or capture is completed. Requires the order ID as a path parameter. Returns the updated order object with abort status. This operation is used to cancel orders in early stages of processing, preventing any payment authorization or capture from occurring. Abort differs from void in that it cancels orders before authorization is obtained.
data.reasoning: Summary is too generic. Description is missing entirely. Enhanced summary for clarity. Added description explaining abort's purpose (canceling before authorization), required inputs, expected output, and the key distinction from void (abort happens before authorization, void after). This helps AI agents understand the order lifecycle and when to use abort versus void.

message: Operation 'retryorder' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, unclear_purpose, poor_ai_interpretability. Summary is too generic and doesn't indicate this is for failed orders. Description is missing entirely. Enhanced summary to clarify this is for failed orders. Added description explaining retry's purpose (recovering from failures), required inputs, expected output, and the operational context (transient failures, customer resolution). This helps AI agents understand when retry is appropriate and what outcome to expect.

data.description_suggestion: Retries a previously failed order transaction, attempting to process payment again. Requires the order ID as a path parameter. Returns the updated order object with the new transaction attempt status. This operation is used to recover from transient payment failures or after customer issues have been resolved. Retry will attempt to re-authorize and capture the order based on its original configuration.
data.reasoning: Summary is too generic and doesn't indicate this is for failed orders. Description is missing entirely. Enhanced summary to clarify this is for failed orders.
